title: Random wobbles in time could finally solve gravity’s greatest mystery
description: The question of how gravity interacts with the quantum world has long perplexed physicists, but a non-quantum theory of space-time could present an answer
author: Leah Crane

title: Geoengineering could expose plane passengers to sulphuric acid
description: A proposed technique to counter global warming by spraying sun-reflecting particles near the poles would cause commercial flights to pass through clouds of sulphuric acid, posing a danger to passengers and crew
author: Syris Valentine

title: What is 'SpudCell'? Arguably the greatest bioengineering feat yet
description: A prototype cell partly capable of replicating itself has been created using 36 existing bacterial genes, but it's not really a living organism – yet
author: Michael Le Page

title: The weirdness of neutrinos could completely rewrite particle physics
description: A philosopher has put forward an argument for rethinking how particles are defined within the standard model of particle physics
author: Karmela Padavic-Callaghan

title: A type of fibre that stimulates GLP-1 release approved for use in food
description: A form of fibre that boosts the release of the hunger-reducing hormone GLP-1 has been approved as safe by a European regulator, and could be added to foods within a year
author: Michael Le Page

title: Slowdown of AMOC ocean current may be gradual and reversible
description: Scientists worry that a surge of meltwater from Greenland could irreversibly collapse the Atlantic Meridional Overturning Circulation, but new modelling suggests the weakening of the current could be reversed if CO2 levels come back down
author: Alec Luhn
